case_name: Hagelauer, K. v. Main Line Hospitals

summary.brief: Hagelauer, K. v. Main Line Hospitals. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: [Mrs. Hagelauer] claimed Defendants negligently failed to evaluate Mr. Hagelauer when he arrived by ambulance at the Paoli Hospital Emergency Department on April 28, 2018, after collapsing and briefly losing consciousness while playing tennis.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Approximately four weeks later, on May 23, 2018, Mr. Hagelauer was again playing tennis when he collapsed and became unresponsive. Efforts to resuscitate Mr. Hagelauer were unsuccessful, and he was taken by ambulance to Chester County Hospital where he was pronounced dead having suffered cardiac arrest.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On February 29, 2024, the jury returned a verdict finding no negligence on the part of any Defendant.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We vacate the judgment and remand for a new trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Dr. Brilliant and [Physician Assistant] Lunardi failed to meet the standard of care by failing to ensure Mr. Hagelauer was admitted to the hospital and evaluated by a cardiologist for his exertional syncope with follow-up testing, including an echocardiogram and stress testing.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court erred in precluding Mrs. Hagelauer from cross-examining Dr. Raja with his own article that conflicts with his testimony during trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.chief_complaint: Collapse and brief loss of consciousness while playing tennis
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Mr. Hagelauer collapsed and briefly lost consciousness while playing tennis on April 28, 2018.

requestables.name: Cardiology Consult
requestables.description: Consultation with a cardiologist for evaluation of exertional syncope

reveal.clinical_significance: Would assess for cardiac causes of syncope
reveal.decision_impact: Could lead to further cardiac testing

requestables.name: Echocardiogram
requestables.description: Ultrasound of the heart to evaluate structure and function

reveal.decision_impact: Important for ruling out cardiac causes

requestables.name: Exercise Stress Test
requestables.description: Test to evaluate cardiac function under stress

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED after collapsing during tennis

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Department Workup
timeline_phases.description: Evaluation and management in the ED

decision_points.prompt: Should the patient be admitted for further evaluation?

options.description: Admit for cardiology evaluation and further testing
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for exertional syncope to rule out serious cardiac issues

options.description: Discharge with advice to follow up with primary care
options.clinical_reasoning: Assumed low risk without further investigation

actual_action_defendant.description: Patient was discharged without cardiology evaluation
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed no serious cause for syncope

expected_action_court.description: Patient should have been admitted for cardiology evaluation
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated need for further evaluation

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to admit for further evaluation of exertional syncope
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Admit for cardiology consult and testing

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered cardiac arrest and died

key_findings: Jury found no negligence on the part of Defendants

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to admit for cardiology evaluation
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Admission could have led to diagnosis and prevention

factual_timeline.event: Mr. Hagelauer collapses while playing tennis and is taken to Paoli Hospital ED
Mr. Hagelauer collapses again and is pronounced dead

allegations.allegation: Failure to admit for cardiology evaluation
